content: agent1: implementing TTL support for DSPy cache (per-entry expiry). Files: dspy/clients/cache.py, dspy/clients/__init__.py. Will add ttl param to configure_cache(), Cache class, set_ttl() helper. Let me know if you're touching these files.

agent1: I've already completed my TTL edits to dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py. Summary of my changes so you can merge safely: (1) cache.py: added 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' as trailing param in Cache.__init__ (after memory_max_entries), stored as self.ttl, memory_cache uses TTLCache(maxsize=..., ttl=ttl) when ttl is not None else LRUCache. (2) put(): disk write now does 'self.disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=self.ttl)' when self.ttl is not None else 'self.disk_cache[key] = value' — this REPLACES the old plain 'self.disk_cache[key] = value' line, so if you're touching put() please rebase on top of my version rather than the original. (3) get(): added 'if self.enable_disk_cache and self.ttl is not None: self.disk_cache.expire()' in the miss branch (the final 'else: return None'). (4) Added Cache.set_ttl(self, ttl) method after load_memory_cache(). (5) __init__.py: configure_cache() got trailing 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' param, passed positionally as 6th arg to Cache(...). DSPY_CACHE now passes ttl=None explicitly. Added module-level set_ttl(seconds) function (calls dspy.cache.set_ttl(seconds)) and added 'set_ttl' to __all__. Please add your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy as trailing kwargs after my ttl param in both Cache.__init__ and configure_cache signatures, and re-read the current file state before editing put()/get()/configure_cache() to avoid clobbering. I've already verified my changes pass tests. Ready to submit patch.txt whenever - let me know when you're done so I can do a final check.
